mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by more than 25 basis points over a 3-month window, the reduction in short-term borrowing costs triggers a compression of equity risk premiums and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.7% suggests limited room for further easing as of 2018-06-15
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum and consumer income expansion, which increases aggregate demand and corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148,662 thousand as of 2018-05-01 indicates strong labor market
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10) when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k persons in preceding month.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, which reduces near-term economic growth expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,287 thousand (revised to 1,276) as of 2018-04-01
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or mean forward return is positive (> 0.2%) when HOUST < 1,200k.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 1.5% year-over-year while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises above 4.0%, signaling a supply-side contraction coupled with labor market softening. This interaction triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.3 (revised to 102.4) and UNRATE at 3.8% as of 2018-05-01
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure or post-shock recovery stall
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, the real federal funds rate becomes negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through lower real discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 250.535 (2.8% YoY) and FEDFUNDS at 1.7% as of 2018-05-01
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with moderate inflation
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 1.0% while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.5% annualized, indicating consumer demand weakness and economic deceleration that compresses equity earnings multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM < -1.0% AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 501,971 million (revised to 491,960) and GDPC1 at 17,379.728 billion as of 2018-05-01 and 2018-01-01
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or mean forward return is positive (> 0.1%) when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence that suppress equity valuations through lower earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% as of 2018-05-01, near historical lows
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 3.0% year-over-year while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 200,000 persons month-over-month, signaling robust supply-side expansion and labor market tightness that supports corporate profit growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth > 3.0% AND PAYEMS MoM growth > 200k persons
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.3 and PAYEMS at 148,662 thousand as of 2018-05-01
regime_hint: strong cyclical expansion with supply-side momentum
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.
